company.name: Intellia Therapeutics
company.subtitle: In vivo CRISPR platform · lonvo-z HAELO topline mid-2026 · nex-z clinical holds LIFTED · BLA H2 2026

assets.name: Lonvo-z (NTLA-2002) — Hereditary Angioedema
assets.stage: Phase 3 HAELO fully enrolled Sep 2025; topline mid-2026; BLA H2 2026; launch 1H27

market.notes: Wholly owned. In vivo CRISPR knock-out of KLKB1 (prekallikrein) gene in liver → lifelong HAE attack reduction after single dose. ~7,000 treated HAE patients US (per Intellia pipeline disclosure Feb 2026). Phase 1/2 showed 96% attack reduction, 31/32 patients attack-free at 50mg dose, three-year durability at AAAAI 2026. HAELO Phase 3: 50mg dose, global, 1:1 vs placebo, enrollment completed Sep 2025 in <9 months. Competes vs Takhzyro (Takeda lanadelumab subcut prophylaxis), Orladeyo (BioCryst oral), Haegarda (CSL), Firazyr. One-time treatment positioning is unique — if durability holds, potential disruptor of $3B+ HAE prophylaxis market. Pricing challenge: gene therapies priced $2-3M/dose, patients need to switch from chronic prophylaxis.

assets.name: Nex-z (NTLA-2001) — ATTR Cardiomyopathy
assets.stage: Phase 3 MAGNITUDE — hold LIFTED Mar 2 2026 after Grade 4 liver event + patient death

market.notes: Partnered with Regeneron (25% cost/profit share, co-promote option US). In vivo CRISPR knock-out of TTR gene in liver → permanent TTR protein reduction after single dose. MAGNITUDE Phase 3: 1,200 patients (expanded from 765), ATTR-CM. Hold imposed Oct 29 2025 after Grade 4 ALT/bilirubin event in a patient dosed Sep 30 2025. Patient died per Q4 earnings call. Hold LIFTED Mar 2 2026 with enhanced liver monitoring mitigations. Competes vs Attruby (Alnylam) approved 2025, Amvuttra (vutrisiran), Vyndaqel/Vyndamax (tafamidis/acoramidis), Wainua (BridgeBio acoramidis). One-time dosing vs chronic RNAi the key differentiator — but liver safety signal is a permanent scar on the thesis.

assets.name: Nex-z (NTLA-2001) — ATTR Polyneuropathy
assets.stage: Phase 3 MAGNITUDE-2 — hold LIFTED Jan 27 2026, enrollment resumed, completion 2H26

market.notes: Partnered with Regeneron (25% cost/profit share). Smaller indication — ~40K patients globally, ~10K US addressable. MAGNITUDE-2 Phase 3 hold lifted Jan 27 2026 (ahead of MAGNITUDE — smaller safety risk profile in PN vs CM given patient comorbidity differences). Enrollment resumption underway globally. Expected completion 2H 2026. Reads out 2027. Competes vs established RNAi (Onpattro/vutrisiran), tafamidis.

mega_bear.narrative: HAELO fails + nex-z safety signal derails ATTR franchise — CRISPR in-vivo thesis broken
events: ✗ HAELO Phase 3 misses primary endpoint or shows durability attrition vs Phase 2
✗ Additional liver safety events in resumed MAGNITUDE/MAGNITUDE-2 trials
✗ FDA requires additional studies → nex-z timeline slips to 2028+
→ Stock retests 52-week low ~$6, company forced to dilute heavily or merge

bear.narrative: HAELO positive but modest commercial ramp; nex-z proceeds with heavy safety monitoring
events: ✓ HAELO hits primary endpoint but effect size smaller than Phase 2 (80-85% attack reduction)
~ HAE launch slow — pricing pressure + durability uncertainty deters switchers
~ MAGNITUDE-2 completes enrollment but readout delayed to late 2027
→ Stock ~$15-18 range, hold on clinical execution

base.narrative: HAELO delivers strong data matching Phase 2; first in vivo CRISPR therapy launches; nex-z proceeds
events: ✓ HAELO Phase 3 hits endpoint with ~90% attack reduction, durable 12+ months
✓ BLA accepted H2 2026, launch 1H 2027 — first in vivo CRISPR therapy
✓ HAE launch ramps to $150-300M over 3 years (~10% share of prophylaxis market)
~ MAGNITUDE enrollment completes, interim data 2027, no new safety signals

bull.narrative: Lonvo-z becomes standard of care + MAGNITUDE readouts de-risk nex-z in both ATTR subtypes
events: ✓ HAELO data matches or exceeds Phase 2 (95%+ attack reduction, 3+ year durability)
✓ Lonvo-z becomes HAE standard of care, peak sales $600M+
✓ MAGNITUDE-2 positive 2027 — first gene editing for neurological ATTR
✓ In vivo CRISPR validated — platform value re-rates (CRSP, BEAM rally with NTLA)

psychedelic_bull.narrative: In vivo CRISPR platform validated + MAGNITUDE CM success + Regeneron or major pharma acquires
events: ✓ Lonvo-z dominates HAE prophylaxis, $800M+ peak, ex-US expansion
✓ MAGNITUDE-CM positive — nex-z peak $2B+ as ATTR-CM standard
✓ Regeneron exercises co-promote + attempts acquisition at $40-60/share
✓ Platform value re-rates — Novartis/Vertex/BMS bid for CRISPR platform

lastData.title: Both nex-z clinical holds lifted; HAELO topline mid-2026 is the near-term binary
points: Mar 2 2026: FDA lifted MAGNITUDE clinical hold (ATTR-CM) — full Phase 3 resumes
Jan 27 2026: FDA lifted MAGNITUDE-2 hold (ATTRv-PN) — smaller trial restarted first
Oct 29 2025 hold trigger: Grade 4 ALT/bilirubin in MAGNITUDE patient dosed Sep 30 2025; patient later died
HAELO Phase 3 (lonvo-z HAE) fully enrolled Sep 2025 in <9 months — topline data by mid-2026
BLA submission for lonvo-z H2 2026; anticipated US launch 1H 2027
3-year durability data presented at AAAAI 2026 for lonvo-z 50mg cohort
Q3 2025 cash $670M, raised $114.5M via ATM in Q3 2025
Runway into mid-2027 — covers HAE launch start
NTLA-3001 discontinued Jan 2025 reorg, 27% workforce cut
William Blair upgraded to Outperform Mar 2 2026 on hold lift; BofA still Hold

nextCatalyst.title: HAELO Phase 3 topline data (lonvo-z in HAE)
thresholds: HAELO primary endpoint: monthly HAE attack rate vs placebo
Phase 2 benchmark: 96% attack reduction at 50mg, 31/32 patients attack-free
Durability: at least 12 months matching Phase 2 (which showed 3+ year durability)
Safety: no liver events (nex-z scare creates sensitivity)
BLA-enabling data package for H2 2026 submission

risks: Nex-z liver safety signal is unresolved — FDA lifted hold with mitigations but mechanism unclear
Patient death in MAGNITUDE is permanent on the record — competitive attack line for Alnylam/BridgeBio
HAE market is crowded — Takhzyro, Orladeyo, Haegarda all established with known profiles
One-time dosing pricing challenge — patients on working chronic prophylaxis reluctant to switch
Durability of CRISPR knockout unproven at 5+ years — key pushback from payors
Cash runway into mid-2027 tight — needs HAE launch execution to avoid bridge financing
Regeneron partnership means 25% of nex-z economics goes away — full platform value capped
Competition from CRSP (exa-cel sickle cell approved), BEAM, Verve (in vivo PCSK9) for platform validation
Phase 3 effect sizes often smaller than Phase 2 (regression to mean risk)
Short interest 40%+ of float — crowd positioning is bearish, but creates squeeze potential
28% decline in shares outstanding + ATM usage = persistent dilution overhang
HAELO data could disappoint even if positive — 80-85% reduction vs 96% Phase 2 would miss bull case
